title: T-020 — API: cycle detection service for wo_dependencies
prompt:
Implement DFS cycle detection per PRD §5.9 + V-PLAN-WO-005. Function `assertNoCycle(parentWoId, childWoId, tx)` reads existing wo_dependencies graph for tenant, simulates the proposed edge, runs DFS with visited+recursion-stack sets, throws `CycleError({ cyclePath: [woId,...], offendingEdge: {parent, child} })` if a cycle would form. Pure-TypeScript implementation; unit-test against in-memory graph fixtures.

RED test: 4 fixtures — linear chain OK, self-loop A→A rejected, cycle A→B→C→A rejected with cyclePath=[A,B,C,A], diamond A→B,A→C,B→D,C→D OK.

pipeline_inputs.description: Pure-TS DFS cycle detection used by cascade generator and dependency-edit endpoints.
pipeline_inputs.details: Algorithm: build adjacency list from existing wo_dependencies for tenant + the candidate edge, run iterative DFS using two sets (visited + onStack), abort + throw on first onStack hit returning cycle path via parent pointer. Avoid recursion (stack overflow on deep chains). cyclePath rebuilt from parent[] map.
acceptance_criteria: Given an empty graph and edge A→A, when assertNoCycle runs, then it throws CycleError with cyclePath=[A,A].
Given graph A→B,B→C and proposed edge C→A, when assertNoCycle runs, then it throws with cyclePath=[A,B,C,A].
Given diamond A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D and any new edge that does not close a cycle, when assertNoCycle runs, then it returns without throwing.
Given `pnpm vitest run src/server/services/__tests__/cycle-detection.test.ts`, when run, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED tests for self-loop, simple cycle, indirect cycle, diamond no-cycle case.
risk_red_lines: Do not use recursive DFS — risk of stack overflow on long chains.
Do not mutate the graph passed in — pure function only.
